center.name: 塔納河
center.summary: 塔納河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黃嘴䴉鸛，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Flock in a seasonal wetland, Zambia The yellow-billed stork occurs primarily in Eastern Africa, but is widely distributed in areas extending from Senegal and Somalia down to South Africa and in some regions of western Madagascar. During one observation of a mixed species bird colony on the Tana River in Kenya, it was found to be the commonest species there, with 2000 individuals being counted at once. It is also the dominant species in large breeding colonies at Lake Manyara and the Ihefu wetlands fringing the Ripera River in Tanzania. The species does not generally migrate far, at least not outside its breeding range; but does usually make short migratory movements which are influenced by rainfall
